name: Halal
description: Permissible under Islamic law. Excludes pork and pork derivatives, alcohol, non-halal slaughtered meat.
cross_contamination_rules: Separate utensils and cooking surfaces required
notes: Halal certification from MUIS (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore). Approximately 15% of Singapore's population is Muslim — halal catering is very common. Continental Pantry supplier is NOT MUIS certified — avoid for halal events.

name: Vegan
description: No animal products of any kind — no meat, dairy, eggs, or honey.
cross_contamination_rules: Ideally separate cooking station for strict vegans
notes: Singapore has a growing vegan scene. Sayur Lodeh, Aglio Olio, Hummus, Popiah, and Mee Goreng (without egg) are vegan-friendly options.

name: Vegetarian
description: No meat, poultry, or seafood. Dairy and eggs permitted.
cross_contamination_rules: Separate serving utensils for veg and non-veg dishes
notes: Common request in Singapore due to Buddhist and Hindu communities. Always offer at least 2 vegetarian mains.

name: Gluten-Free
description: No wheat, barley, rye, or any gluten-containing grains.
cross_contamination_rules: Dedicated cooking surfaces and utensils. Cross-contamination risk is high.
notes: Chicken Rice, Satay, Rendang, Laksa (with rice noodles), and most Malay curries are naturally gluten-free.

name: Jain
description: Vegetarian + no root vegetables (onion, garlic, potato, carrot). No eating after sunset traditionally.
notes: Rare in Singapore but occasionally requested by Indian expatriate community. Significant constraint — limited menu options.

name: Nut-Free
description: No tree nuts or peanuts due to allergy risk. Critical safety requirement.
cross_contamination_rules: CRITICAL — complete separation. Label all dishes clearly.
notes: Singapore schools and many corporate offices have strict nut-free policies. Satay and Nasi Lemak contain peanuts — flag as HIGH RISK for nut-free events.

name: Diabetic-Friendly
description: Low glycemic index foods. Avoid high sugar, refined carbs, fried foods.
notes: Singapore has one of the highest diabetes rates in Asia. Fresh Tropical Fruit Platter is the go-to dessert for diabetic-friendly events.

name: Keto
description: High fat, moderate protein, very low carbohydrate. No grains, starchy vegetables, or sugar.
notes: Chicken Satay, Chili Crab Bites, Caesar Salad, and Mixed Green Salad are keto-compatible.
